name: El Faro Cabo de San Antonio — circular by Jávea
name_original: El Faro Cabo de San Antonio — circular por Jávea
region: Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni offer diverse terrain, characterized by dramatic coastal cliffs that drop into the Mediterranean Sea. The region is an extension of the Montgó Natural Park, featuring rocky cliffs, Mediterranean scrub, and panoramic sea views. This protected area provides a variety of routes suitable for exploring its unique terrestrial and marine landscapes. Best easy hiking trails in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni The most popular easy hiking route is Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic windmills atop the cape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plana de Sant Jeroni loop from Reserva Natural dels Fons Marins del Cap de Sant Antoni, an easy 3.9 miles (6.2 km) path. This trail offers views across the plateau and towards the coast. Local hikers also love the Arenal Beach – Playa de l'Arenal loop from Xàbia / Jávea, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 11 minutes. Hiking in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, Mediterranean scrub, and panoramic sea views. The network offers options for different ability levels, with many easy hiking trails. The routes in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 980 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: There are over 240 easy hiking trails available in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni, offering a wide variety of options for all skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly easy hikes in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: Yes, the region offers many easy trails suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle terrain and stunning coastal views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, exploring historical sites with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see along the easy hiking trails?
A: The easy trails in this natural reserve offer dramatic coastal scenery, including cliffs plunging into the Mediterranean Sea. You can also find unique plant micro-reserves and observe the diverse flora and fauna of the Montgó Natural Park. Notable natural highlights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt; and various &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5931859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic vistas of the coast and sea.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in the Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: Yes, there are several circular easy walks. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana de Sant Jeroni loop from Reserva Natural dels Fons Marins del Cap de Sant Antoni&lt;/a&gt;, which provides views across the plateau and towards the coast. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites can be explored on easy hikes?
A: The region is rich in history. You can visit the remains of old cylindrical windmills, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The historic lighthouse atop the cape, in use since 1855, also offers a beautiful viewpoint and insight into the area's past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada&lt;/a&gt; has historical elements dating back to the 11th-12th centuries.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in this region?
A: The Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni is enjoyable for easy hikes throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended for coastal walks, taking advantage of the sea breeze.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails near Denia?
A: Yes, the Cap de Sant Antoni is located between Jávea and Denia, offering easy trails with proximity to both towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33757336" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sea View from Dénia Cliffs – Torre del Gerro loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent easy option that starts near Denia and provides stunning coastal vistas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 980 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic sea views, the dramatic coastal cliffs, and the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable and accessible walks.

Q: Is parking available for easy hiking trails in the area?
A: Parking is generally available at various access points for trails, especially near popular starting locations like the Cap de Sant Antoni lighthouse or trailheads leading to Cova Tallada. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information, particularly during peak seasons.

Q: Can I see wildlife on easy hikes in Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni?
A: Yes, the diverse flora and fauna of the Montgó Natural Park, which extends to the cape, make it a good spot for observing wildlife. You might encounter various bird species, reptiles, and endemic plants along the trails, especially in quieter areas away from the main viewpoints.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to beaches or coves?
A: Absolutely. Some easy trails lead to or offer views of the region's beautiful beaches and coves. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40365064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arenal Beach – Playa de l'Arenal loop from Xàbia / Jávea&lt;/a&gt; is an easy coastal walk that includes the popular Arenal Beach. You can also find trails that provide access to smaller, more secluded coves.

Q: What makes the landscape of Reserva Natural Dels Fons Marins Del Cap De Sant Antoni unique for easy hiking?
A: The landscape is unique due to its dramatic cliffs that drop up to 175 meters into the Mediterranean, offering unparalleled sea views. It's an extension of the Montgó Mountain, combining rocky terrain with Mediterranean scrub. The strong 'Llebeig' wind has also shaped the landscape, contributing to its distinctive character, making for a varied and visually stunning hiking experience.
tips: From this viewpoint you will have the best views of Jávea and Denia! A unique place with amazing views, ideal throughout the day but especially recommended at sunrise or sunset!

The lighthouse that overlooks the bay from Cap de Sant Antoni was the successor to other defensive structures that took advantage of the privileged location of the plain. Hermits built the chapel of San Antonio in the 14th century to isolate themselves from secular life. It was built in his honor and eventually gave the relief its name. A watchtower to protect against pirate raids was also built on this ground, which later gave way to the current lighthouse. This viewpoint is without a doubt one of the most privileged locations on the Valencian coast.

Nice cove just a few meters from the Marina of Jávea. It is accessed by a path with beautiful views. It is usually quite calm, although in season it is usual to cross people both on the road and in the cove itself.

waypoints.name: View of Jávea, Cape Sant Antoni, and Montgó
